codeReview.summary: The worker successfully fixed the Supabase profile auto-creation trigger that was blocking signups. The fix involved creating a new migration (006) that recreates the handle_new_user function with three key improvements: (1) coalesce() handling for null raw_user_meta_data to prevent null pointer errors, (2) exception handler that logs errors as warnings without blocking user creation, and (3) modern 'execute function' syntax instead of deprecated 'execute procedure'. The migration was applied via `supabase db push --linked`, and manual verification confirmed that new user signups now work correctly without the 'Database error finding user' message.

issues.description: The trigger now uses coalesce(new.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name', '') which will set full_name to an empty string when metadata is missing. This is an improvement over the previous version that would fail on null, but consider whether a null value would be more semantically correct than an empty string in the database.

The exception handler catches all errors with 'when others' and logs them. This is good for ensuring user creation doesn't block, but it means profile creation failures are silent. Consider logging to a dedicated error table or using Supabase's internal logging for better observability in production.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that Supabase CLI requires `supabase link` to be run before using `supabase db push`. The `supabase link --project-ref {ref}` command was used successfully. This command pattern could be documented in .factory/services.yaml or .factory/library/ under a 'Database Migrations' section to help future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'supabase link --project-ref rypjlvcazpgeompyebbw' succeeded, then 'supabase db push --linked' was used to apply migrations. The services.yaml currently only documents web service start/stop, not database migration workflows.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill's procedure mentions 'Database migrations via SQL (apply through Supabase dashboard or MCP)' but the Supabase MCP tools don't have access to the CareerAC project (only 'passionseed' project is accessible). The worker had to use Supabase CLI with `supabase link` and `supabase db push` instead. The skill documentation should note this alternative approach when MCP access is unavailable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'The Supabase MCP only gives access to a different project (passionseed), not the CareerAC project (rypjlvcazpgeompyebbw)'. Worker eventually used 'supabase link --project-ref rypjlvcazpgeompyebbw' and 'supabase db push --linked' successfully.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The supabase/config.toml generated by `supabase init` contains extensive configuration including local development ports that don't match the production setup. Future workers should be aware that this file is primarily for local Supabase development (which uses Docker) and may need different handling than the cloud-based approach documented in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: config.toml includes settings for local ports (54321, 54322, 54323) that are not used since the app uses Supabase Cloud at rypjlvcazpgeompyebbw.supabase.co. The file was committed but may cause confusion if workers try to start local Supabase.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The profile trigger issue was caused by the original migration (003) using deprecated 'execute procedure' syntax instead of 'execute function', and not handling null raw_user_meta_data. This pattern (auth triggers failing silently) is a common Supabase pitfall that could be documented in .factory/library/ as a troubleshooting guide.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Migration 003 used 'execute procedure public.handle_new_user()' (deprecated) while 006 uses 'execute function public.handle_new_user()' (modern). The original also didn't have coalesce() handling for null metadata, which caused failures when users signed up without full_name in metadata.

summary: The fix-supabase-profile-trigger feature has been successfully implemented. The worker identified the root cause (trigger function not handling null metadata and using deprecated syntax), created migration 006 with proper error handling (coalesce for nulls, exception handler to prevent blocking), and applied it to the remote database. Manual verification confirmed that signup now works correctly: new users are created, profiles are auto-inserted, and the 'Database error finding user' error no longer appears. The implementation is clean, follows PostgreSQL best practices for triggers, and includes proper defensive programming (exception handling). All validators pass (94 tests passing, TypeScript clean, lint clean) with only 1 pre-existing unrelated test failure.
